_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House HD124-128 (San Antonio/Bexar + NW/SE Houston): josey-garcia (D, anti-school-choice + pro-LGBTQ), ray-lopez (D, RETIRED not running), sam-harless (R, RETIRING + 2A raise-the-age deviation), charles-cunningham (R, 96% TAL pro-life + pro-school-choice), briscoe-cain (R, LOST TX-9 runoff, hardline record). State tier.

profile.background: U.S. Air Force veteran (20 yrs); former foster child; cofounder of Uniting America Outreach (San Antonio relief nonprofit); St. Mary's University School of Law. TX House HD124 since 2023.

josey-garcia@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: D incumbent, won uncontested 2026 Democratic primary, faces Republican Sylvia Soto in Nov 2026 general. Texas Alliance for Life 89th (2025) score 48% (mixed). Texas Taxpayers Fiscal Index F/32 (career F). Voted NO on SB 2 (2025 ESA school-choice, House RV ~86-61, all Democrats opposed). Openly bisexual (self-identified). Framed by local press as police-reform activist. No documented conservative-aligned positions found.

0.note: Openly bisexual legislator; does not affirm marriage as exclusively the union of one man and one woman.

1.note: Openly LGBTQ Democrat; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

0.note: Voted NO on SB 2 (2025), the Education Savings Account / school-choice program; all present House Democrats opposed.

profile.background: Career San Antonio public servant: 9 yrs Northside ISD board, 8 yrs (4 terms) San Antonio City Council, TX House HD125 since 2019 special election.

ray-lopez@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: CANDIDACY CURRENCY -- announced 2025-08-18 he will NOT seek reelection to a 5th term (retiring; age 75); seat is open, Democrat Adrian Reyna won the 2026 Democratic runoff. Still seated through Jan 2027. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes. Voted NO on SB 2 (2025 ESA school choice); longtime ISD trustee, ATPE-backed. Joined the Aug 2025 House Democrat quorum break against GOP congressional redistricting. No documented conservative-aligned positions found.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (abortion-industry endorsement).

0.note: Voted NO on SB 2 (2025 ESA / school-choice program); longtime ISD trustee endorsed by education establishment.

profile.background: Co-owns Fred Fincher Motors with wife Patricia (former HD126 rep 2007-2016). Sam Houston State University. Chaired House Corrections Committee (89th). TX House HD126 since 2019.

sam-harless@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: CANDIDACY CURRENCY -- retiring, NOT seeking reelection; seat open, Stan Stanart won the 2026 GOP primary runoff (May 26, 2026). Still seated through Jan 2027. Relative fiscal moderate: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility Fiscal Index 63 (2025), career grade F. DEVIATION: in 2023 (88th) was one of only two Republicans to vote in committee to advance HB 2744 raising the rifle-purchase age 18->21 after the Allen mall shooting (a gun-control measure). Voted YES on SB 2 (2025 ESA school choice) -- a reversal from his 2021 anti-voucher budget vote. Voted YES on HB 1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry).

0.note: Voted YES on SB 2 (2025) Education Savings Account / school-choice program (reversal from prior anti-voucher position after Abbott endorsement).
Voted YES on HB 1927 (2021), Texas permitless/constitutional carry (House 87-58).

1.note: DEVIATION: one of two House Republicans to vote in committee to advance HB 2744 (2023) raising the age to purchase semi-automatic rifles from 18 to 21 -- a gun-control restriction.

profile.background: U.S. Army veteran; Our Lady of the Lake University; former CenterPoint Energy. 12 yrs Humble ISD board, 4 yrs Humble City Council. Elected to TX House HD127 in the regular Nov 2022 election (succeeding the retiring Dan Huberty), seated Jan 2023.

charles-cunningham@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: R incumbent (2nd term), running in Nov 2026 general vs Democrat Michelle Williams. Note: seated via regular 2022 election, NOT a 2023 special election. Texas Alliance for Life 89th (2025) score 96% (strongly pro-life); backed SB 8 Heartbeat Act. Voted YES on SB 2 (2025 ESA school choice); endorsed by Gov. Abbott in 2024 primary as a voucher supporter; filed HB 1176 charter expansion. Relative fiscal moderate -- Fiscal Responsibility Index career grade F (74 in 2025). DATA FIX: prior record's claim he voted for constitutional carry is impossible (HB 1927 passed 2021, before his 2023 term) -- cleared to null.

0.note: 96% Texas Alliance for Life (2025) record; backed SB 8 Texas Heartbeat Act -- affirms protection of life from early gestation.

1.note: Supported SB 8 Texas Heartbeat Act and maintains a 96% pro-life voting record.

0.note: Voted YES on SB 2 (2025) ESA / school-choice program; Abbott-endorsed voucher supporter; filed HB 1176 charter expansion.

profile.background: Attorney (South Texas College of Law JD); former House Freedom Caucus member; chaired House Elections Committee (2021). Hardline conservative. TX House HD128 2017-2027.

briscoe-cain@TX.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: CANDIDACY CURRENCY -- did NOT seek reelection to HD128; filed for U.S. House TX-9 (Aug 2025) and LOST the GOP primary runoff to Alex Mealer (May 26, 2026). Leaving the Legislature; holds no 2026 ballot office -> status lost. Texas Right to Life 100% pro-life record every year in office; authored an early version of the Texas Heartbeat Act (SB 8) and sent cease-and-desist letters to abortion funds. Texans for Fiscal Responsibility 2025 score 96 ('Taxpayer Champion'), career A+. Chaired House Elections Committee (2021) and was the lead House proponent of the 2021 election-integrity overhaul (SB 1) restricting mail-in voting. Voted YES on SB 2 (2025 ESA school choice).

0.note: 100% Texas Right to Life record every year in office; affirms life from conception.

1.note: Authored an early version of the Texas Heartbeat Act (SB 8) and sent cease-and-desist letters to abortion funds.

4.note: 100% Texas Right to Life record and TRTL PAC endorsement; no abortion-industry money.

0.note: Voted YES on SB 2 (2025) ESA / school-choice program.

2.note: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility 2025 score 96 ('Taxpayer Champion'), career A+ -- consistent fiscal restraint / opposition to deficit spending.

1.note: Chaired House Elections Committee (2021) and led the election-integrity overhaul; supports voter-ID requirements.

3.note: Lead House proponent of the 2021 SB 1 overhaul restricting mail-in voting and ballot assistance.
